result.summary: Adds input validation to the parser and reorders the cache eviction path so flush runs after unlink.
result.risk_surface: Reordered eviction may lose the entry if a crash lands between unlink and flush.

findings.title: Reordered eviction path has no covering test
findings.explanation: No test exercises evict() after the flush/unlink reorder; cache.py coverage falls below the 80% threshold.
findings.fix: Add a test pinning unlink-then-flush ordering.

findings.title: Stale evict() docstring describes the old ordering
findings.explanation: The docstring still says flush-before-unlink; the body now unlinks first.
findings.fix: Update the docstring to the new ordering.

findings.title: Early-return guard breaks the module's single-exit style
findings.explanation: validate() now mixes an early return with the trailing return the file otherwise uses.

findings.title: flush() after _unlink() forces a second list traversal
findings.explanation: Flushing a node already unlinked re-walks the LRU list to locate neighbors.
findings.fix: Capture the node before unlinking and flush it directly.

findings.title: Empty payload short-circuits sanitization
findings.explanation: validate('') returns None, so downstream sanitization never runs for empty-but-crafted payloads that later coerce to strings.
findings.fix: Raise on empty input instead of returning None.

findings.title: Eviction unlinks the entry before persisting it
findings.explanation: evict() now unlinks the node before flush(), so a crash between the two loses the write.

findings.title: Unused import textwrap
findings.explanation: textwrap is imported but never referenced.

findings.title: Eviction unlinks the entry before persisting it
flush() after _unlink() forces a second list traversal
Early-return guard breaks the module's single-exit style
Stale evict() docstring describes the old ordering
Reordered eviction path has no covering test

result.rationale: The diff adds no test file; the reordered path is untested.
The docstring is not visible in the diff hunks; cannot confirm from diff evidence alone.
The file already uses early returns in parse(); the guard matches house style.
_unlink() returns the node; flush(key) hits the dict, not the list — no second traversal.
validate('') returns None and no caller in the diff guards it before the sink; escalating.
The added import line has no matching use in the diff.
The hunk moves flush(key) after _unlink(key); the loss window is real.

result.summary: 2 blockers (eviction loss window; empty-payload sanitization bypass), 1 major test gap, 2 refuted, 1 needs a human look.
result.report_markdown:
## Review — request changes

### Blockers
- **COR-1** src/cache.py:40 — eviction unlinks before persisting; crash window loses the write.
- **SEC-1** src/parser.py:88 — empty payload short-circuits sanitization (escalated).

### Major
- **TC-1** src/cache.py:38 — reordered eviction path has no covering test.

### Needs human
- **DOC-1** src/cache.py:38 — stale evict() docstring (not provable from the diff).

### Nits
- **COR-2** src/parser.py:1 — unused import textwrap.
